name: Chocolate-Glazed Triple Layer Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: (8 1/2 oz) pkg. chocolate wafer cookies, crushed (about 2 cups)
2 (8 oz) pkgs. cream cheese, softened and divided
1/3 cup dark brown sugar, firmly packed
recipeInstructions:
1. Crush the chocolate wafer cookies and mix with 1/4 cup sugar and melted butter in a medium bowl. Press into the bottom and up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan. Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, combine one package of cream cheese and 1/4 cup sugar. Beat until fluffy. Add 1 egg and 1/4 teaspoon vanilla; blend well. Stir in melted chocolate and 1/3 cup sour cream.
3. Pour over the chocolate crust.
4. In another bowl, combine the remaining package of cream cheese, 1/3 cup brown sugar, and flour. Beat until fluffy. Add 1 egg and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla; blend well. Stir in pecans.
5. Spoon gently over the chocolate layer.
6. Combine 5 ounces cream cheese and the remaining 1/4 cup sugar. Beat until fluffy. Add 1 egg; blend well. Stir in the remaining sour cream, 1/4 teaspoon vanilla, and almond extract.
7. Spoon gently over the praline layer.
8. Bake at 325 degrees Fahrenheit for 1 hour. Turn off the oven and let it cool in the on position for 30 minutes. Open the oven door and continue to cool with the oven turned off for an additional 30 minutes. Cool completely, then chill for 8 hours. Remove from the pan and spread warm chocolate glaze over the cheesecake. Garnish with chocolate leaves if desired.
